content: Some general comments first before I take a more in-depth review: * Formatting of actions: Please use and tags, e.g. MOVE dir. * Formatting of HP and AP values: Consider formatting the values using , e.g. 40 HP. * End conditions: Please use separate sections for Victory and Defeat. You may refer to the page below as a reference: https://www.codingame.com/ide/puzzle/git-patchwork * Input sections: Please add "space-separated" or "separated by a space" to all applicable lines. * Constraints: Please show one constraint per line. * Cover picture: Consider adding a cover picture for the puzzle. * Viewer: Are you going to add an opening sequence? Thanks.

Hello,

For some reason the opening demo was working with the Main test class provided, but it seems not to be working with the exported project.

I have to investigate further in order to understand why.

I took into account most of your feedback about the statement. I hope it's better now.

I fixed the Demo that was not showing. But surprisingly, the Referee seems not to be able to output within the 10000ms alloted time.

I can't find yet why it is working to generate the Demo but not to allow a player on the platform to run a test case...

Another puzzle ( https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/101457c36421540967e67263c5d29e9a89179a ) once suffered from a large replay size, but I don't know if that's the case here. Just offering a wild guess.

I'm not an expert here, so if necessary you may seek advice on CodinGame's Discord chat.

I'll review your contribution again within the next few days.

Found the answer on the discord. The gameengine version was too low (4.0.0 instead of 4.5.0) and the score parsing function migrated from a string parameter to a double paremeter, making the compilation of the Referee crash.

It's all working now. :)

Glad to hear that the issue was resolved quickly! Below are some suggestions and clarification questions after reviewing the statement. Goal * Please change "6 first turns" to "first 6 turns". Rules - Resources * Please change "per turn, restored" to "initially, and AP is restored in full". Rules - Available actions * Please add a blank line before the heading "Available actions". * Please change "" to "dir". * In PASS, please add "pending" after "turns". * The statements • They can also perform at most 2 jumps per turn. • Each turn, you send exactly one action. appear contradictory. The former implies that multiple actions per turn are possible (two JUMP actions), while the latter suggests only one action per turn. Please clarify the intended behaviour. Rules - Effects of a movement * Please change "Any movement costs 1 HP" to "The character moves and loses 1 HP." * Does this mean that if a character with 1 HP moves onto a cell containing a frame, they would die (because movement costs 1 HP) before the HP cost is refunded (if applicable)? Please clarify the behaviour. * If the bullet points describe the exact sequence of events, please add at the beginning: The following events happen in the following order: Otherwise please arrange the bullet points in the correct order and add the above text. * Please add ", keeping the same relative direction from the character" after "pushed one cell away from the character", if that accurately describes the push behaviour. * Please clarify under what conditions a flame cannot move. * Please clarify how attraction works when a flame isn't aligned with one of the eight compass directions relative to the character. Which cell does it move to in that case? * Is it possible for two flames to be pushed or attracted to the same cell? If so, is overlap allowed, or are neither moved? Please clarify the intended behaviour. Rules - Flame spawning * Please change "6 first turns" to "first 6 turns". * "glyphs appear": Do you mean "new glyphs appear"? * Consider removing the middle sentence describing how the referee chooses glyph sequences, since the selection mechanism doesn't affect player decisions; players get the pending glyph positions each turn. Of course, if the intention is for players to study the referee logic for offline optimisation, then I'll discuss with you how that should be mentioned or handled further. * Please clarify whether pending glyphs for a turn appear in the immediate next turn. Missing rules * The mechanism describing how IMMO affects flame movement is currently unclear. Please consider adding a section explaining this behaviour in greater detail. * Please specify the coordinate system used in the grid: • Is (0, 0) located at the top-left or bottom-left corner? • In which directions do the x and y coordinates increase? (reached character limit…)

(…continued)

Defeat conditions / Constraints
* If glyphs only convert to flames when PASS is executed, a player could theoretically avoid activating them by never passing. Is there a maximum number of turns or another mechanic preventing this? If so, please add the maximum number of turns to the Constraints section and include a relevant defeat condition.

Initialization input
* Line 1: Please add "space-separated" before "integers".

* Please change "The next" to "Next".

* Please add "of length <var>width</var>" after a string.

Input for each turn
* Please change "The next" to "Next" (twice).

* Please add "space-separated" before "integers":
  • Line 1
  • Line 2
  • Next flameCount lines
  • Next glyphCount lines

Output
* Please change "<dir>" to "dir".

Constraints
* Please merge the constraints for charX, flameX and glyphX:

0 ≤ <var>charX</var>, <var>flameX</var>, <var>glyphX</var> < <var>width</var>

* Similarly, please merge the constraints for charY, flameY and glyphY.

Cover picture
* Consider adding a cover picture for the puzzle.

Cases and testing
* I'll review these later.

Thanks.
